---
title: "MTH 4330 project draft"
subtitle: "Test Code for Group Project"
author: "Raymond Cheung"
date: "2026-03-31"
output: pdf_document
header-includes:
# Add any LaTeX packages you want here -----------------------
- \usepackage{amsmath}
- \usepackage{amssymb}
- \usepackage{graphicx}
---



<!-- Keep this block  -->
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)

# Add any R packages you want here -----------------------
library(rstudioapi)

library(dplyr) #added

library(xtable) #added to export latex tables

# Do not touch below this line ---------------------------
setwd(dirname(rstudioapi::getActiveDocumentContext()$path))
def.chunk.hook  <- knitr::knit_hooks$get("chunk")
knitr::knit_hooks$set(chunk = function(x, options) {
  x <- def.chunk.hook(x, options)
  paste0("\n \\", "footnotesize","\n\n", x, "\n\n \\normalsize")
})
```

```{r random_forest, echo = TRUE}

# this entire thing is taken from Random_forest.R from the GitHub. Slight modification: centering fiscal year

library(data.table)
library(ranger)

payroll <- data.table::fread(
  "prorated_annual_Citywide_Payroll_Data_(Fiscal_Year)_20260227.csv",
  select = c(
    "Fiscal Year",
    "Regular Gross Paid",
    "Total OT Paid",
    "Base Salary",
    "Regular Hours",
    "OT Hours",
    "Pay Basis"
  )
)

payroll <- payroll[toupper(trimws(`Pay Basis`)) == "PRORATED ANNUAL"]

payroll$`Regular Gross Paid` <- as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.-]", "", as.character(payroll$`Regular Gross Paid`)))
payroll$`Total OT Paid` <- as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.-]", "", as.character(payroll$`Total OT Paid`)))
payroll$`Total OT Paid`[is.na(payroll$`Total OT Paid`)] <- 0

payroll$Total_Paid <- payroll$`Regular Gross Paid` + payroll$`Total OT Paid`

payroll$`Fiscal Year` <- as.numeric(as.character(payroll$`Fiscal Year`)) - 2014 # centered at 2014 = 0
payroll$`Regular Hours` <- as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.-]", "", as.character(payroll$`Regular Hours`)))
payroll$`Base Salary` <- as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.-]", "", as.character(payroll$`Base Salary`)))
payroll$`OT Hours` <- as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.-]", "", as.character(payroll$`OT Hours`)))

payroll <- payroll[, c(
  "Fiscal Year",
  "Regular Hours",
  "Base Salary",
  "OT Hours",
  "Total_Paid"
)]

payroll <- na.omit(payroll)

names(payroll) <- c("Fiscal_Year", "Regular_Hours", "Base_Salary", "OT_Hours", "Total_Paid")

set.seed(42)

n <- nrow(payroll)

# 70/15/15 split
train_idx <- sample(seq_len(n), size = floor(0.70 * n))
remaining <- setdiff(seq_len(n), train_idx)
dev_idx <- sample(remaining, size = floor(0.15 * n))
test_idx <- setdiff(remaining, dev_idx)

train <- payroll[train_idx, ]
dev   <- payroll[dev_idx, ]
test  <- payroll[test_idx, ]

# random forest model (copied from the GitHub)

rf_model <- ranger::ranger(
  Total_Paid ~ Fiscal_Year + Regular_Hours + Base_Salary + OT_Hours,
  data = train,
  num.trees = 200,
  max.depth = 12,
  min.node.size = 5,
  seed = 42
)

# dev set R^2

dev_pred <- predict(rf_model, data = dev)$predictions

dev_r2 <- 1 - sum((dev$Total_Paid - dev_pred)^2) /
  sum((dev$Total_Paid - mean(dev$Total_Paid))^2)

cat("development R^2:", round(dev_r2, 6), "\n")

# main model metrics

pred_rf <- predict(rf_model, data = test)$predictions

mae_rf <- mean(abs(test$Total_Paid - pred_rf))
rmse_rf <- sqrt(mean((test$Total_Paid - pred_rf)^2))

r2_rf <- 1 - sum((test$Total_Paid - pred_rf)^2) /
  sum((test$Total_Paid - mean(test$Total_Paid))^2)

cat("MAE:", round(mae_rf, 3), "\n")
cat("RMSE:", round(rmse_rf, 3), "\n")
cat("R^2:", round(r2_rf, 6), "\n")

# Random Forest actual vs predicted plot

plot(
  test$Total_Paid,
  pred_rf,
  xlab = "Actual Total Paid",
  ylab = "Predicted Total Paid (Random Forest)",
  main = "Random Forest: Actual vs Predicted Total Paid",
  pch = 16,
  col = "blue"
)

abline(0, 1, col = "red", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
```
